spec_match.rationale: The page now frames Chrome 139 as removing HTML ISO-2022-JP auto-detection while preserving explicit charset declarations, matching the feature and HTML encoding-determination behavior.

interactive.rationale: The byte sample, declaration source, charset selector, and sample button all drive live Chrome 138 versus Chrome 139 parser-decision output.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept still matches the feature index promise: paste ISO-2022-JP-like bytes, declare or omit a charset, and compare Chrome 138 and Chrome 139 outcomes.

edge_cases.rationale: Controls now cover missing charset, explicit UTF-8, explicit ISO-2022-JP, wrong charset, matching header/meta declarations, header/meta conflicts, and non-HTML MIME boundaries.

references.rationale: The see-also section now cites chromestatus.com/feature/6576566521561088 and the HTML encoding-determination section.

design_system.rationale: The added controls and verdict panels use shared CSS variables, pair local button colors, and keep warning/ok badge text contrast readable.

summary: The major ChromeStatus reference blocker and the missing edge branches are addressed. The script now parses, user-provided bytes are rendered with text nodes instead of injected HTML, and the guide explains the security motivation with explicit, conflicting, missing, and non-HTML declaration paths. Chrome DevTools MCP was not available in this session, so no browser-verification claim is made here.
